items.summary: A NSW hospital becomes the first in Australia to offer a new stroke-prevention treatment that has been available in the United States for a decade.

items.title: El Niño in 2026 could be one of the strongest on record — what does it mean for Australia's weather?
items.summary: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly, thanks to the probable emergence of an El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires could be on the way.

items.title: More than 250 missing after boat with Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals sinks in Andaman Sea
items.summary: The UN says the boat sank in the Andaman Sea with about 250 people on board, including children, "due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding".

items.title: Ombudsman report into interaction between Queensland Health and boys with disabilities makes 13 recommendations
items.summary: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as tourists can no longer afford fuel to travel
items.summary: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

items.title: Horsham Town Hall postpones performances after hidden cameras found in change rooms
items.summary: An investigation into hidden cameras in change rooms has forced the Horsham Town Hall to postpone events in western Victoria.

items.title: Should you turn off your laptop while commuting?
items.summary: While modern laptops are good at saving battery life and keeping cool, extended periods in sleep mode could still be putting a strain on them over time.

items.title: Undertone's director taps into our fear of things that go bump in the night
items.summary: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

items.title: Travel quiz, April 15: We're going on a trip — don't forget your toothbrush
items.summary: What are the pyramids made out of? And which Caribbean island doesn't appear in the Beach Boys song Kokomo?

items.title: Trump lashes out at European ally Meloni amid tensions over pope and NATO
items.summary: The US president says Giorgia Meloni "does not care" about Iran getting a nuclear weapon, after the Italian PM defended the pope and cancelled a military deal with Israel.

items.title: IMF warns of inflation surge, growth slump if Strait of Hormuz disruptions continue
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund paints three economic outlook scenarios that each get progressively worse depending on how long Middle East energy supplies are disrupted.

items.title: Perth man on trial for alleged rape, assault of several women
items.summary: Benjamin Bruce, 26, appeared before the WA District Court on charges including sexual penetration without consent and assault against several women.

items.title: Earthquake south of Orange shakes homes over hundreds of kilometres
items.summary: The NSW Central West has been hit by the largest earthquake ever recorded in the region.

items.title: Cartel Paradise: A special investigation into the Pacific's drug superhighway
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: WA government offers FluMist nasal spray vaccine free to children
items.summary: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

items.title: Humpback whale freed after being stranded on sandbar at Wallis Lake on NSW mid north coast
items.summary: A whale stranded on a sandbar in Wallis Lake on the NSW mid north coast has been freed by marine animal rescue teams.

items.title: St Kilda forward Lance Collard receives nine-week suspension over homophobic slur to VFL opponent
items.summary: St Kilda forward Lance Collard is suspended after a marathon AFL Tribunal hearing with a deliberation of over 90 minutes.

items.title: Man behind four threatening hate letters to Lakemba Mosque, politicians given corrections order
items.summary: A man who sent threatening letters to a Sydney mosque was "drinking himself to sleep" and "fell into some type of internet rabbit hole", a court hears.

items.title: Inquiry hears Parkes school expects to lose students if waste incinerator is built
items.summary: A school in the middle of a $22-million master plan for its growing enrolment numbers is worried the buildings will not be needed if a waste to energy plant is approved in the town.

items.title: Coalition's new migration plan faces practical questions
items.summary: The Coalition's new migration policy aims to increase screening of visa applicants and "boot out" people who do not abide by Australia's values, but questions remain about how the plan would work in practice.

items.title: Court hears alleged planning behind 2023 cafe shooting of underworld figure Gavin Preston
items.summary: Prosecutors allege two men were chauffeured back to NSW after conducting a contract killing of the Melbourne underworld figure in 2023.

items.title: Residents near Adelaide cement plant raise concerns about more plastic being burnt for fuel
items.summary: Residents living near a large cement plant in Adelaide are concerned about the owner wanting to burn more plastic as fuel to replace natural gas.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein to face retrial on New York rape charge
items.summary: Former Hollywood magnate Harvey Weinstein is again going on trial on a rape charge in New York City. He will remain imprisoned for other offences regardless of the verdict.

items.title: Zak Butters asked 'why did you pay that?' Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says
items.summary: Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says the AFL club will back Zak Butters "to the hilt" amid dispute over what was said to an umpire during Sunday night's loss against St Kilda.

items.title: Court told 'hundreds' of croc eggs allegedly stolen from Kakadu by Matt Wright's former pilot
items.summary: Four men who were allegedly involved in a "sophisticated" operation to steal "hundreds" of crocodile eggs from Kakadu National Park have faced court in Darwin.

items.title: Sydney community radio station 2SER faces uncertain future as university funding dries up
items.summary: 2SER, which was jointly owned by the University of Technology Sydney and Macquarie University, has given many aspiring broadcasters their start in the industry.

items.title: Fresh warning about using superannuation on health treatments after 100 complaints to regulator
items.summary: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

items.title: Butters found guilty of abusing umpire as Port claims 'glaring hole in the evidence'
items.summary: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters is found guilty of umpire abuse at the AFL Tribunal, despite strongly denying allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?".

items.title: Wanted man found injured, detained by Bray Park residents, dies in hospital
items.summary: A man wanted by NSW Police dies in hospital from injuries sustained after being detained by two men near Murwillumbah.

items.title: NSW energy minister says 'difficult trade-offs' necessary in New England Renewable Energy Zone
items.summary: The New South Wales energy minister has met with disgruntled landowners affected by the New England Renewable Energy Zone.

items.title: Financial, environmental and safety issues emerge at Nathan River Resources' Roper Bar mine
items.summary: Nathan River Resources has stood down more than 40 workers since Good Friday. Now financial, environmental and safety issues at the mine are being raised, as the NT government is accused of weak regulation.

items.title: Colombia approves plan to cull roaming hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
items.summary: Colombian officials have authorised a plan to cull dozens of hippos, years after notorious drug lord Pablo Escobar brought in the first ones.

items.title: Man charged over alleged murder of Hayley McClenahan-Ernst in Sydney's west
items.summary: The former partner of Hayley McClenahan-Ernst has been charged with her murder, nine-years after the pregnant mother's suspicious death in Sydney's west.

items.title: Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre could be converted into prison
items.summary: The WA government flags the possible conversion of the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre into a prison amid an "unprecedented" rise in the number of inmates being crammed into cells across the state.

items.title: Stagflation is a 'central banker's nightmare', says RBA deputy governor
items.summary: RBA deputy governor Andrew Hauser says stagflation is a "central banker's nightmare" and the coming months will be challenging for Australia.

items.title: Wallaroos forced to take refuge inside US hotel amid approaching tornado
items.summary: The Wallaroos' preparations for the Pacific Four Series are dealt an unexpected blow as a warning alarm sounds inside the team hotel in Kansas City.

items.title: WA government says it has secured 4 million litres of diesel
items.summary: The diesel is set to arrive in Western Australia "in the coming weeks" as part of a strategic stockpile to address acute, localised fuel shortages across the state — but it's already been dismissed as "woefully inadequate".

items.title: Scientists confirm skull found in Gippsland cave a century ago belongs to giant echidna
items.summary: A fossil hidden in a museum collection for 119 years has revealed giant echidnas once roamed Victoria's east, filling a major gap in Australia's prehistoric record.

items.title: INXS is out, Phil Collins in as Rock & Roll Hall of Fame 2026 inductees announced
items.summary: INXS has missed out on landing a place in the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame with 2026 inductees including Phil Collins, Queen Latifah and Fela Kuti.

items.title: Man accused in Molotov cocktail attack on OpenAI CEO's home charged with attempted murder
items.summary: A man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at the home of OpenAI's CEO is charged with attempted murder. Authorities say he had written about AI's purported risk to humanity and "our impending extinction".

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan begin first leg of Australia visit in Melbourne
items.summary: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have begun their multi-day visit to Australia in Melbourne, meeting with patients at a children's hospital.

items.title: The Zak Butters and Nick Foot case has shown the AFL needed to protect its umpire from conflict of interest concerns
items.summary: The AFL gambled that concerns over a conflict of interest with one of its umpires wouldn't be an issue, but that call has come back to bite them — and it's not the umpire's fault.

items.title: States to reveal if they support privatisation of Big Bash League clubs, with possible IPL part-ownership
items.summary: Cricket Australia will know in the coming days if the states are happy to privatise their Big Bash League clubs, a call that may see them become part-owned by Indian Premier League franchises.

items.title: Police weigh up charges over alleged Islamophobic attack
items.summary: Police say if there is sufficient evidence, a man accused of attacking people at a community dinner could be charged under hate speech laws.

items.title: How Wests Tigers veteran Alex Twal defied the odds to transform his NRL career
items.summary: For years, Alex Twal was best known for the things he didn't do but, after helping the Tigers surge up the ladder, the cult hero forward has become one of the most improved players in the NRL.

items.title: How I've learnt to love my asymmetrical body after cancer
items.summary: I decided to keep my healthy breast, opting against reconstruction. It means I'm now what's known in the community as a uniboober.

items.title: Trailblazing former NT Labor senator Trish Crossin dies aged 70
items.summary: A former Arnhem Land schoolteacher who blazed a trail as the Northern Territory’s first female senator, Trish Crossin, has died at age 70.

items.title: Super Netball round-up: Expansion pushed back and the growing trend to call greats out of retirement
items.summary: Two new teams were expected to join the league in 2027 to make Super Netball a 10-team competition, but slow broadcast negotiations are holding up the process.

items.title: Fuel crisis hits WA tourist route as Jetstar suspends Sydney-Busselton flights
items.summary: Rising fuel costs due to the war in Iran see Qantas suspend Jetstar flights between Sydney and Busselton.

items.title: Sydney summers have increased in length by nearly 50 days since 1990, study finds
items.summary: For every 10 years since 1990, Sydneysiders have experienced an extra 15 days of summer, new research has found.

items.title: Therapeutic Goods Administration issues safety alert amid explosion of illegal peptide use
items.summary: The medicines regulator issues a health alert about the risks of using unregulated peptides amid a surge in the use of potentially unsafe injectables.

items.title: From gay rights to prisoner voting, the High Court helped decide the way we live and love
items.summary: From love and freedom to native title and the right to vote, these are some of the High Court cases that have helped define our nation, and the ordinary people behind them.

items.title: Qantas cancels flights between Adelaide and Mount Gambier amid fuel costs
items.summary: Qantas will no longer fly to South Australia's largest regional city, citing fuel costs and declining demand for flights to Mount Gambier.

items.title: Australian paceman Mitchell Starc named leading men's cricketer of the year by Wisden
items.summary: Australia's Ashes ace Mitchell Starc is named the leading men's cricketer of the year by the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack after taking 55 wickets in 2025.

items.title: Ukulele players strum their way to better mental health
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Sunshine Coast ukulele club's 600 members may be strumming their way to better health
items.summary: From a small gathering to a packed RSL, this Queensland ukulele club has found its rhythm, and a brain researcher says playing could offer its members a multitude of health benefits.

items.title: Gout Gout's 200m record showed a maturing runner, but Australian fans must be patient
items.summary: Gout Gout was on the lips of every athletics fan across the globe on the weekend, but elements of his record-breaking run need to be treated with caution.

items.title: Police thank Nannup community after 87-year-old bushwalker rescued
items.summary: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

items.title: California Democrat Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual assault allegations
items.summary: Eric Swalwell, a California Democrat, says he will resign from Congress following multiple sexual assault allegations.

items.title: St Kilda's Lance Collard to receive AFL sanction after being found guilty of homophobic slur
items.summary: St Kilda forward Lance Collard will learn via an AFL hearing how heavy a suspension he will receive after being found guilty of using a homophobic slur towards a VFL opponent.

items.title: Electronics seized after reports of hidden cameras in Horsham Town Hall change rooms
items.summary: Victorian police have seized electronic devices after reports of hidden cameras in change rooms at Horsham Town Hall.

items.title: Man in custody after resident stabbed multiple times in Gold Coast home invasion
items.summary: A man is recovering in hospital after being stabbed during an early morning home invasion.

items.title: Child sex attack survivor asks home affairs minister to deport abuser back to UK
items.summary: A girl who was sexually assaulted in Perth by a UK citizen when she was nine years old writes to Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, pleading with him to cancel the man's visa after he avoided deportation on appeal.

items.title: Philippines accuses China of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
items.summary: Manila has accused China of poisoning waters around a ship where it has marines stationed, but Beijing has dismissed the allegations.

items.title: NT Indigenous mining royalty fund AINT's investment strategy questioned
items.summary: Indigenous people in the NT are being offered the chance to tap into a $680-million royalties fund, but some analysts worry the money is being spent too cautiously.

items.title: Cleaner stumbles across major drug, explosives and cash haul at Melbourne rental property
items.summary: Authorities were forced to conduct controlled explosions at a property in Melbourne's north-west after a cleaner stumbled across drugs and explosives.
